tenders.title: Auto-injectors for PET-CT Service
tenders.description: This procurement is for the acquisition of auto-injector systems to support PET-CT services across NHS Scotland. The aim is to standardize the delivery of radiopharmaceuticals, improve patient safety, enhance imaging quality, and streamline clinical workflows. This procurement will apply to the 4 NHS Boards involved in PET-CT imaging, NHS GG&C, NHS Lothian, NHS Grampian and NHS Tayside. The procurement is to do a 10 year, non-framework contract as this covers the lifespan of the machines. The procurement will also cover the maintenance of the machines for the lifespan and the delivery of all consumables required for the daily operation of the machines. It will run alongside the PET-CT replacement programme. There will be a single award as the requirements of the service are to standardise equipment and delivery.

tenders.title: HH072-25-HB Specialist Environmental Aids for Deaf People
tenders.description:
NHS Highland is seeking to engage a suitably qualified organisation to deliver a comprehensive service for the provision of environmental aids and equipment designed to enhance the independence, safety, and overall wellbeing of individuals with hearing loss across the Highland region.
 The appointed contractor will be responsible for the following key service components:
 Supply of Aids and Equipment: Provision of specialist environmental aids and equipment to eligible individuals, on a loan basis, as determined by NHS Highlandâs established risk and eligibility criteria.
 Maintenance and Repair Services: Facilitation of repairs to issued equipment where feasible, ensuring that returned items are restored to a functional condition suitable for re-issue, thereby supporting the sustainability and efficiency of the service.
 Recycling and Disposal Compliance: Management of equipment recycling and disposal in accordance with the Waste Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE) regulations, ensuring environmentally responsible practices are upheld.
 Training and Advisory Support: Delivery of expert guidance and training to deaf support staff, covering the operation, functionality, and appropriateness of the aids and equipment provided, to ensure optimal use and benefit for individuals with hearing loss.

tenders.title: NP56326 Service for Peritoneal Dialysis Treatments
tenders.description:
The provision of a homecare delivery service to enable patients within NHS Scotland to self-administer peritoneal dialysis treatments at home. The service includes the supply of sterile dialysate fluids dispensed in accordance with each individual patient's prescription as detailed within the specification contained within the tender documentation.
 This tender is for a maximum of 200 patients across Scotland resulting in approximately 2000 deliveries per annum. The number of patients and deliveries will vary through the duration of the framework agreement.
 This Lot will be awarded as an unranked multi supplier framework to a maximum of two (2) suppliers. Full details of the products can be found within the ITT documents. The provision of a homecare delivery service to enable patients within NHS Scotland to self-administer peritoneal dialysis treatments at home. The service may include the supply of a dialysis machine, consumables as required, sterile dialysate fluids dispensed in accordance with each individual patient's prescription, ancillaries as required, the homecare delivery service to the patient at home, or other agreed delivery point, and the removal of waste if required as detailed within the specification contained within the tender documentation.

tenders.title: Central Venous Catheters
tenders.description:
We require a Coated Central Venous Catheter (CVC) which will minimise the risk of infection and is for use in, but not limited to; administering medication or fluids that are unable to be taken by mouth or would harm a smaller peripheral vein, to obtain blood tests and to measure central venous pressure. We require a Non-Coated Central Venous Catheter (CVC) which will minimise the risk of infection and is for use in, but not limited to; administering medication or fluids that are unable to be taken by mouth or would harm a smaller peripheral vein, to obtain blood tests and to measure central venous pressure. We require a Paediatric Central Venous Catheter (CVC) for use in, but not limited to; administering medication or fluids that are unable to be taken by mouth or would harm a smaller peripheral vein, to obtain blood tests and to measure central venous pressure. We require a Midline Catheter for longer term IV access which is ideal for patients whose treatment is expected to last more than three (3) days. We require a Skin Tunnelling Catheter suitable for long term insertion for use in, but not limited to; taking a blood sample, giving medication, giving blood or blood products, giving chemotherapy, intravenous feeding, bone marrow transplantation, and photopheresis. We require a (Peripherally Inserted Central Catheter) PICC Line (Antimicrobial), which is used for a prolonged period of time for Chemotherapy or Antibiotic therapy along with the administration of substances that should not be done peripherally. We require a (Peripherally Inserted Central Catheter) PICC Line (Non-Coated), which is used for a prolonged period of
 time for Chemotherapy or Antibiotic therapy along with the administration of substances that should not be done peripherally. We require a Seldinger arterial lines used in the Seldinger technique of inserting a catheter over a guidewire to safely place a tube into an artery for continuous blood pressure monitoring and blood sampling. We require a Non-Seldinger Arterial Cannulas used in an acute care setting. We require IV Flush Saline Pre-filled Syringes used in a healthcare settings for flushing and maintaining indwelling vascular access devices, like, PICC lines, and central venous catheters. We require Stabilisation Devices used within a clinical healthcare setting. We require a Dialysis Central Venous Catheter which will provide immediate vascular access for haemodialysis, used either temporarily or long-term depending on patient needs. Accessories - Unscored Lot.
 This Lot is for items which don't fall into Lots 1-14 but are used in association with an item(s) submitted in another Lot. These items must be relevant to any of the items within Lots 1-14 of the framework. The Authority reserves the right to accept or decline any items submitted into this Lot onto the framework. Suppliers are only awarded to this Lot if they have been successfully awarded to one of the scored Lots(1-14). Innovation - Unscored Lot.
 For items which are yet to be made available to the market, but will become available over the duration of the framework. These items must be relevant to any of the items within Lots 1-14. The Authority reserves the right to accept or decline any items submitted into this
 Lot onto the framework. Suppliers are automatically awarded to this Lot if they have been successfully awarded to any of the other Lots(1-14). We require Implantable Ports used within a clinical healthcare setting. We require Central Venous Catheters Insertion Packs used in a clinical healthcare setting.
 This is an unscored Lot and suppliers are not automatically awarded to this Lot. To be awarded to this Lot suppliers must first meet all mandatory requirements and tendered against any of the scored Lots within this tender.

tenders.title: NP86626 - Swab Collection Devices
tenders.description:
VTM is supplied in a colour free plastic (polypropylene) tube containing a prefilled level of transport medium. VTM is intended to be used for the collection and preservation and/or transportation of clinical specimen (e.g. mucus, sputum, vaginal secretions, faeces, urine) collected from any body part for in vitro diagnostic procedures. Sample Collection Kit 1ml fill VTM
 IVD Kit contains prefilled tube containing 1ml of VTM with green screw cap and sterile swab with Breakpoint.
 Sample Collection Kit 3ml fill VTM
 IVD Kit contains prefilled tube containing 3ml of VTM with green screw cap and sterile swab with Breakpoint. Individual Tubes containing Inactivation Medium
 Tube containing 2ml pre-filled level of Inactivation Medium, with RED screw cap.
 Inactivation medium is supplied in a colour free plastic (polypropylene) tube containing a prefilled level of inactivation medium. Inactivation medium is intended to be used for the collection and preservation and/or transportation of clinical specimen (e.g. mucus, sputum, vaginal secretions, faeces, urine) collected from any body part for in vitro diagnostic procedures. Inactivation medium will KILL (inactivate) both viral and bacterial pathogens. Sample Collection Kit (tube + swab), containing Inactivation Medium
 Supplied in IVD Peel Pouch containing 2ml fill of Inactivation medium, with RED screw cap and Sterile dry swab with Breakpoint A Sterile swab is a medical device used to collect specimens from any body part for in vitro diagnostic purposes.
 The swab will have a flexible polystyrene shaft with viscose, floxsynthetic or flocked, mini tip, colour white, PCR ready and sterile, with a scored break point and fits securely (once broken) in the tube using a method of capturing the swab securely within the screw cap of tube.
 The swab will be supplied in a peel pouch format and marked with CE number or equivalent UKCA, IVD, storage conditions, sterile, manufacture, part number, lot number and expiry date as a minimum, supplied individually or as part of an IVD PCR Sample collection peel pouch kit.
 Swabs will be used to collect viral and bacterial targets from various orifices of the human body, such As:
 Nasal, throat, ear, cervix, nasopharyngeal, skin, toe nails, vaginal and urethral. This lot captures a range of miscellaneous products, which may be required for use across NHS Scotlandâs Laboratory Estate. The range of products contained within this lot are as follows,
 Item 1 	1 Litre bottle inactivation medium (lysis buffer)
 Item 2 	Replacement blue screw caps
 Item 3 	7ml tube (empty) and loose yellow caps
 Item 4 	Individual Sterile packaged dry swabs without break point. - A Sterile swab is a medical device used to collect specimens from any body part for in vitro diagnostic purposes. The swab will have a flexible polystyrene shaft with viscose, flosynthetic or flocked, mini tip, colour white, PCR ready and sterile, with a scored break point and fits securely (once broken) in the tube using a method of capturing the swab securely within the screw cap of tube.
 The swab will be supplied in a peel pouch format and marked with CE number or equivalent UKCA, IVD, storage conditions, sterile, manufacture, part number, lot number and expiry date as a minimum, supplied individually or as part of an IVD PCR Sample collection peel pouch kit.
 Swabs will be used to collect viral and bacterial targets from various orifices of the human body, such As:
 Nasal, throat, ear, cervix, nasopharyngeal, skin, toe nails, vaginal and urethral.
 Item 5 	Transport pack UN3373 compliant for various tube sizes â sealed bag for internal transportation.
 Item 6	Transport pack UN3373 compliant for various tube sizes â sealed bag with absorption pad for internal and external transportation by courier or Royal Mail.
 Item 7 	Transport pack UN3373 compliant for various tube sizes â rigid container for use in securely transporting by courier or Royal Mail. Material optional,
